content.claim: Hungary's annual birth numbers have collapsed from a peak of 93,000 in 2021 to 72,000 in 2025 (22% decline), with total fertility rate falling to 1.31 children per woman, the lowest in 14 years. Any cohorts entering school from 2028 onward will be materially smaller than cohorts a decade earlier, independent of special educational needs policy.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Based on preliminary 2025 data; future fertility trends remain uncertain
Mechanical demographic fact does not determine policy response or enrollment outcomes
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
